hints.concrete: Check tens first using the place value blocks.
hints.strategy: If tens are tied, compare ones.
hints.worked: 32 vs 38: both have 3 tens, so compare ones, and 2 is less than 8.

hints.concrete: Use a place-value chart for hundreds, tens, and ones.
hints.strategy: Compare hundreds first. If tied, compare tens.
hints.worked: Line up digits by place value and compare step by step.

hints.concrete: Use a place-value chart for tenths/hundredths.
hints.strategy: Compare tenths first, then hundredths.
hints.worked: Rewrite with equal decimal places and compare digits.

hints.concrete: Draw fraction strips with equal-sized wholes.
hints.strategy: Find common denominators or compare to benchmark 1/2.
hints.worked: Convert each fraction to a common denominator and compare.

hints.concrete: Partition the whole into equal groups first.
hints.strategy: Find one part, then scale by numerator.
hints.worked: Compute whole ÷ denominator, then multiply by numerator.

hints.concrete: Build rows and columns with counters.
hints.strategy: Use repeated addition from the array.
hints.worked: Count one row, then multiply by number of rows.

hints.concrete: Use counters and take away one at a time.
hints.strategy: Start at the big number and count back.
hints.worked: If it says 9 minus 4, count back four: 8, 7, 6, 5.

hints.concrete: Use a ten-frame or number line to see the jumps.
hints.strategy: Make ten first, then subtract the rest.
hints.worked: 14 minus 6 can be 14 minus 4 equals 10, then minus 2 equals 8.

hints.concrete: Place long rods in Tens and little cubes in Ones.
hints.strategy: Two-digit numbers are tens plus ones.

hints.concrete: Think of layers of unit cubes.
hints.strategy: Find area of one layer, then multiply by height.

hints.concrete: Point to each dot as you count.
hints.strategy: Start from one corner and count in order.
hints.worked: Count each dot: 1, 2, 3... the answer is the last number you say.

hints.concrete: Use a ten frame to fill in the missing part.
hints.strategy: Think: what number plus the given number equals 10?
hints.worked: If one part is 7, count up from 7 to 10: 8, 9, 10 — that's 3 more.

hints.concrete: Use your fingers or counters to add.
hints.strategy: Count on from the bigger number.

hints.concrete: Use a ten frame to show both groups.
hints.strategy: Count on from the bigger number.

hints.concrete: Use a number line to hop forward.
hints.strategy: Make a ten first, then add the rest.

hints.concrete: Use a number bond triangle.
hints.strategy: Think: whole minus known part = missing part.
hints.worked: For 6 + ? = 9: 9 - 6 = 3, so the missing part is 3.

hints.concrete: Use base-ten blocks: tens sticks and ones cubes.
hints.strategy: Add ones first, then tens. Regroup if ones sum is 10 or more.

hints.concrete: Use base-ten blocks. Trade a ten for 10 ones if needed.
hints.strategy: Subtract ones first. If you can't, regroup a ten.
hints.worked: For 72 - 45: Can't do 2 - 5, so regroup: 6 tens 12 ones. 12 - 5 = 7, 6 - 4 = 2. Answer: 27.

hints.concrete: Line up counters side by side to see which row is longer.
hints.strategy: Count each group and compare the totals.

hints.concrete: Draw the shape and count each side with your finger.
hints.strategy: Sides are straight lines. Corners are where two sides meet.

hints.concrete: Use a hundred chart and hop forward.
hints.strategy: Add tens first, then ones.

hints.concrete: Start at the bigger number and hop back.
hints.strategy: Subtract tens first, then ones.

hints.concrete: Line up objects end-to-end with no gaps and count.
hints.strategy: Measure means counting equal-size units from end to end.

hints.concrete: Use place-value blocks: flats=hundreds, rods=tens, units=ones.
hints.strategy: The hundreds digit tells how many 100s, tens digit tells how many 10s.

hints.concrete: Stack numbers. Add ones first — trade 10 ones for 1 ten if needed.
hints.strategy: When ones add to 10+, carry 1 ten.

hints.concrete: Use base-ten blocks. Trade 1 ten for 10 ones if you need more ones.
hints.strategy: If top ones < bottom ones, borrow from tens.

hints.concrete: Draw circles for groups. Put dots inside each.
hints.strategy: Skip count by the group size.

hints.concrete: Draw coins: dime=10¢, nickel=5¢, quarter=25¢, penny=1¢.
hints.strategy: Count quarters first, then dimes, nickels, pennies.

hints.concrete: Draw a bar for each choice. Tallest bar = most popular.
hints.strategy: Add to find total. Subtract to find how many more.

hints.concrete: Draw circles for groups. Deal objects one by one.
hints.strategy: Think: what times divisor = dividend?

hints.concrete: Fold a strip into equal parts. Each part is one unit fraction.
hints.strategy: 1/n of a number means divide by n.

hints.concrete: Draw fraction strips same length. Shade each fraction and compare.
hints.strategy: Same denominator: bigger numerator wins. Different: find common denominator.

hints.concrete: Cover the rectangle with unit tiles. Count all tiles.
hints.strategy: Area = length × width.

hints.concrete: Draw a picture for each step. Cross out or circle to show changes.
hints.strategy: Break into two steps. Solve first, use that answer for the second.

hints.concrete: Write in a place-value chart with columns for each place.
hints.strategy: Each place is 10× the place to its right.

hints.concrete: Break the larger factor into tens and ones. Multiply each, then add.
hints.strategy: Area model: split into tens×ones, add partial products.

hints.concrete: Subtract friendly multiples of the divisor, one chunk at a time.
hints.strategy: Take away 10×, 5×, 2× and add up quotients.

hints.concrete: Use fraction strips. Shade first, then shade more (add) or cross off (subtract).
hints.strategy: Same denominator: add/subtract numerators, keep denominator.

hints.concrete: Place a protractor on the angle. Read where the ray points.
hints.strategy: Right=90°, straight=180°, full=360°. Acute<90°, obtuse>90°.

hints.concrete: Draw fraction bars same length. Find a common size to compare.
hints.strategy: Find LCD. Convert both fractions, then add/subtract numerators.

hints.concrete: Each dot on the line plot is one data value. Count them.
hints.strategy: Add fraction values for totals. Subtract smallest from largest for range.

hints.concrete: Draw a table with two rows. Fill in the pattern and extend.
hints.strategy: Find the multiplier and apply to both quantities.

rewards.description: Awarded for completing Number Bonds to 10
